description: A rigid, wide-mouthed vessel used to keep food or other small material. A jar often takes a lid, though its exact closure and preservation method remain separate. A bottle pours through a narrow neck; 'tapulo' opens wide enough for a hand or spoon.
sound_symbolism: The opening is the full sound of 'tapu' (close), which recalls the well-fitted closure many jars carry. Rounded 'lo' leaves a broad hollow space beneath it.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'ha tapulo morisa phelu.' (This jar holds salt.) Ordinary phrases give its contents, material, and closure. Preservation safety depends on condition, closure, and method rather than the noun.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The household jar kept salt near the hearth and grain from mice. Another jar carried preserves through the cold months.

tags.creation: a rigid storage vessel with a broad mouth
tags.physical: a holder reached through an opening wider than a bottle neck
